title: ProgramBench: Can Language Models Rebuild Programs From Scratch?
data:
J Yang, K Lieret, J Ma, P Thakkar, D Pedchenko- arXiv preprint arXiv, 2026
Turning ideas into full software projects from scratch has become a popular use case 
for language models. Agents are being deployed to seed, maintain, and grow 
codebases over extended periods with minimal human oversight. Such settings
author: Thanh Le-Cong

title: SieveFL: Hierarchical Runtime-Aware Pruning for Scalable LLM-Based Fault Localization
data:
M Farzandway, F Ghassemi- arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.13491, 2026
Automated fault localization requires connecting an observed test failure to the 
responsible method across thousands of candidates--a task that purely statistical 
approaches handle with limited precision and that LLMs cannot yet handle at full
author: Thanh Le-Cong

title: SmellBench: Evaluating LLM Agents on Architectural Code Smell Repair
data:
IG Dinu, MC Mihescu, T Rebedea- arXiv preprint arXiv:2605.07001, 2026
Architectural code smells erode software maintainability and are costly to repair 
manually, yet unlike localized bugs, they require cross-module reasoning about 
design intent that challenges both developers and automated tools. While large
